rank_rationale: #1: ages from 6 months, 46 reviews, chunky posting fit.
include_rationale: Busy hands just past two want to match a shape and try again when it sticks. A clear birdhouse sorter keeps that try visible without tiny fiddly pieces. Best first sorter when they are past pure banging and into posting shapes on the floor.

rank_rationale: #2: related busy hands; second IKEA slot; 380 reviews.
include_rationale: Shape work is not only posting through holes. Nesting beakers let them sort by size and tip them out for another go on the floor. Second IKEA busy hands set when the sorter is already on the shelf at home.

product_name: Melissa and Doug Shape Sorting Cube
rank_rationale: #3: Ages 2+ wooden classic; Smyths smoke OK and buyable.
include_rationale: A wooden cube sorter is the familiar UK shelf staple for matching shapes by hand at this age. Chunky pieces fit just past two better than tiny plastic sets. Good when you want a sturdy wooden cube instead of a birdhouse form.

product_name: Melissa and Doug Chunky Pets Puzzle
rank_rationale: #4: chunky lift out puzzle; second MD brand slot.
include_rationale: Shape sorting is one busy hands job; lift out puzzles are the next step for just past two. Chunky pet pieces let them lift, name and reseat without tiny tabs. Second Melissa and Doug slot when the cube is already sorted.

A second chunky farm puzzle brand keeps ownership flexible if Melissa and Doug is already on the shelf. Large pieces fit busy hands just past two. Good gift when you know they already enjoy lift out animal pieces at home.
